title: An R Package for Density Ratio Estimation
text: Density ratio estimation described follows: given two data samples x1x1 x2x2 unknown distributions p(x)p(x) q(x)q(x) respectively, estimate w(x)=p(x)q(x) w(x) = \frac{p(x)}{q(x)} x1x1 x2x2 dd-dimensional real numbers. estimated density ratio function w(x)w(x) can used many applications anomaly detection [Hido et al. 2011], change-point detection [Liu et al. 2013], covariate shift adaptation [Sugiyama et al. 2007]. useful applications density ratio estimation summarized [Sugiyama et al. 2012]. package densratio provides function densratio() returns object method estimate density ratio compute_density_ratio(). example, function densratio() estimates density ratio p(x)p(x) q(x)q(x), $$ w(x) = \frac{p(x)}{q(x)} = \frac{\rm{Norm}(1, 1/8)}{\rm{Norm}(1, 1/2)} $$ provides function compute estimated density ratio. densratio object function compute_density_ratio() can compute density ratio ŵ(x)≃p(x)/q(x)\hat{w}(x) \simeq p(x)/q(x) dd-dimensional input xx (d=1d=1).  case, true density ratio $w(x) = p(x)/q(x) = \rm{Norm}(1, 1/8) / \rm{Norm}(1, 1/2)$ known. can compare w(x)w(x) estimated density ratio ŵ(x)\hat{w}(x).
code: set.seed(3) x1 <- rnorm(200, mean = 1, sd = 1/8) x2 <- rnorm(200, mean = 1, sd = 1/2)  library(densratio) densratio_obj <- densratio(x1, x2) new_x <- seq(0, 2, by = 0.05) w_hat <- densratio_obj$compute_density_ratio(new_x)  plot(new_x, w_hat, pch=19) true_density_ratio <- function(x) dnorm(x, 1, 1/8) / dnorm(x, 1, 1/2)  plot(true_density_ratio, xlim=c(0, 2), lwd=2, col="red", xlab = "x", ylab = "Density Ratio") plot(densratio_obj$compute_density_ratio, xlim=c(0, 2), lwd=2, col="green", add=TRUE) legend("topright", legend=c(expression(w(x)), expression(hat(w)(x))), col=2:3, lty=1, lwd=2, pch=NA)

title: An R Package for Density Ratio Estimation
text: can install densratio package CRAN. can also install package GitHub. source code densratio package available GitHub https://github.com/hoxo-m/densratio.
code: install.packages("densratio") install.packages("remotes") # If you have not installed "remotes" package remotes::install_github("hoxo-m/densratio")

title: An R Package for Density Ratio Estimation
text: package provides densratio(). function returns object function compute estimated density ratio. data samples x1 x2, case, densratio_obj$compute_density_ratio() can compute estimated density ratio.
code: library(densratio)  x1 <- rnorm(200, mean = 1, sd = 1/8) x2 <- rnorm(200, mean = 1, sd = 1/2)  result <- densratio(x1, x2) new_x <- seq(0, 2, by = 0.05) w_hat <- densratio_obj$compute_density_ratio(new_x)  plot(new_x, w_hat, pch=19)

title: An R Package for Density Ratio Estimation
text: densratio() method argument can pass "uLSIF", "RuSLIF", "KLIEP". uLSIF (unconstrained Least-Squares Importance Fitting) default method. algorithm estimates density ratio minimizing squared loss. can find information [Kanamori et al. 2009] [Hido et al. 2011]. RuLSIF (Relative unconstrained Least-Squares Importance Fitting). algorithm estimates relative density ratio minimizing squared loss. can find information [Yamada et al. 2011] [Liu et al. 2013]. KLIEP (Kullback-Leibler Importance Estimation Procedure). algorithm estimates density ratio minimizing Kullback-Leibler divergence. can find information [Sugiyama et al. 2007]. methods assume density ratio represented linear model: w(x)=θ1K(x,c1)+θ2K(x,c2)+...+θbK(x,cb) w(x) = \theta_1 K(x, c_1) + \theta_2 K(x, c_2) + ... + \theta_b K(x, c_b) K(x,c)=exp⁡(−∥x−c∥22σ2) K(x, c) = \exp\left(-\frac{\|x - c\|^2}{2 \sigma ^ 2}\right) Gaussian (RBF) kernel. densratio() performs following: Decides kernel parameter σ\sigma cross-validation, Optimizes kernel weights θ\theta (words, find optimal coefficients linear model), parameters σ\sigma θ\theta saved densratio object, used compute density ratio call compute_density_ratio().

title: An R Package for Density Ratio Estimation
text: can display information densratio objects. Moreover, can change conditions specify arguments densratio(). Kernel type fixed Gaussian. Number kernels number kernels linear model. can change setting kernel_num argument. default, kernel_num = 100. Bandwidth (sigma) Gaussian kernel bandwidth. default, sigma = "auto", algorithm automatically select optimal value cross validation. set sigma number, used. set sigma numeric vector, algorithm select optimal value cross validation. Centers centers Gaussian kernels linear model. selected random data sample x1 underlying numerator distribution p(x)p(x). can find whole values result$kernel_info$centers. Kernel Weights theta parameters linear kernel model. can find values result$kernel_weights. Function Estimate Density Ratio named compute_density_ratio().
code: densratio_obj ##  ## Call: ## densratio(x1 = x1, x2 = x2, method = "uLSIF") ##  ## Kernel Information: ##   Kernel type:  Gaussian  ##   Number of kernels:  100  ##   Bandwidth (sigma):  0.1  ##   Centers:  num [1:100, 1] 0.907 1.093 1.18 1.136 1.046 ... ##  ## Kernel Weights: ##   num [1:100] 0.067455 0.040045 0.000459 0.016849 0.067084 ... ##  ## Regularization Parameter (lambda):  1  ##  ## Function to Estimate Density Ratio: ##   compute_density_ratio()

title: An R Package for Density Ratio Estimation
text: far, input data samples x1 x2 one dimensional. densratio() allows input multidimensional data samples matrix, long dimensions . example, case, well, can compare true density ratio estimated density ratio.
code: library(densratio) library(mvtnorm)  set.seed(3) x1 <- rmvnorm(300, mean = c(1, 1), sigma = diag(1/8, 2)) x2 <- rmvnorm(300, mean = c(1, 1), sigma = diag(1/2, 2))  densratio_obj_d2 <- densratio(x1, x2) densratio_obj_d2 ##  ## Call: ## densratio(x1 = x1, x2 = x2, method = "uLSIF") ##  ## Kernel Information: ##   Kernel type:  Gaussian  ##   Number of kernels:  100  ##   Bandwidth (sigma):  0.316  ##   Centers:  num [1:100, 1:2] 1.257 0.758 1.122 1.3 1.386 ... ##  ## Kernel Weights: ##   num [1:100] 0.0756 0.0986 0.059 0.0797 0.0421 ... ##  ## Regularization Parameter (lambda):  0.3162278  ##  ## Function to Estimate Density Ratio: ##   compute_density_ratio() true_density_ratio <- function(x) {   dmvnorm(x, mean = c(1, 1), sigma = diag(1/8, 2)) /     dmvnorm(x, mean = c(1, 1), sigma = diag(1/2, 2)) }  N <- 20 range <- seq(0, 2, length.out = N) input <- expand.grid(range, range) w_true <- matrix(true_density_ratio(input), nrow = N) w_hat <- matrix(densratio_obj_d2$compute_density_ratio(input), nrow = N)  par(mfrow = c(1, 2)) contour(range, range, w_true, main = "True Density Ratio") contour(range, range, w_hat, main = "Estimated Density Ratio")

title: Density Ratio Estimation
text: Density ratio estimation described follows: given two data samples x1 x2 unknown distributions p(x) q(x) respectively, estimate w(x) = p(x) / q(x), x1 x2 d-dimensional real numbers. estimated density ratio function w(x) can used many applications anomaly detection [Hido et al. 2011], change-point detection [Liu et al. 2013], covariate shift adaptation [Sugiyama et al. 2007]. useful applications density ratio estimation summarized [Sugiyama et al. 2012]. package densratio provides function densratio() returns object method estimate density ratio compute_density_ratio(). example, densratio object function compute_density_ratio() can compute estimated density ratio w_hat(x) d-dimensional input x (now d=1).  case, true density ratio w(x) = p(x)/q(x) = Norm(1, 1/8) / Norm(1, 1/2) known. can compare w(x) estimated density ratio w-hat(x).
code: set.seed(3) x1 <- rnorm(200, mean = 1, sd = 1/8) x2 <- rnorm(200, mean = 1, sd = 1/2)  library(densratio) densratio_obj <- densratio(x1, x2) new_x <- seq(0, 2, by = 0.03) w_hat <- densratio_obj$compute_density_ratio(new_x)  plot(new_x, w_hat, pch=19) true_density_ratio <- function(x) dnorm(x, 1, 1/8) / dnorm(x, 1, 1/2)  plot(true_density_ratio, xlim=c(0, 2), lwd=2, col="red", xlab = "x", ylab = "Density Ratio") plot(densratio_obj$compute_density_ratio, xlim=c(0, 2), lwd=2, col="green", add=TRUE) legend("topright", legend=c(expression(w(x)), expression(hat(w)(x))), col=2:3, lty=1, lwd=2, pch=NA)

title: Density Ratio Estimation
text: package provides densratio(). function returns object function compute estimated density ratio. data samples x1 x2, case, densratio_obj$compute_density_ratio() can compute estimated density ratio.
code: set.seed(3) x1 <- rnorm(200, mean = 1, sd = 1/8) x2 <- rnorm(200, mean = 1, sd = 1/2)  library(densratio) densratio_obj <- densratio(x1, x2) new_x <- seq(0, 2, by = 0.03) w_hat <- densratio_obj$compute_density_ratio(new_x)  plot(new_x, w_hat, pch=19)

title: Density Ratio Estimation
text: densratio() method argument can pass "uLSIF", "RuLSIF", "KLIEP". uLSIF (unconstrained Least-Squares Importance Fitting) default method. algorithm estimates density ratio minimizing squared loss. can find information [Kanamori et al. 2009] [Hido et al. 2011]. RuLSIF (Relative unconstrained Least-Squares Importance Fitting). algorithm estimates relative density ratio minimizing squared loss. can find information [Yamada et al. 2011] [Liu et al. 2013]. KLIEP (Kullback-Leibler Importance Estimation Procedure). algorithm estimates density ratio minimizing Kullback-Leibler divergence. can find information [Sugiyama et al. 2007]. methods assume density ratios represented linear model: w(x) = theta_1 * K(x, c_1) + theta_2 * K(x, c_2) + ... + theta_b * K(x, c_b) K(x, c) = exp(-||x - c||^2 / (2 * sigma^2)) Gaussian (RBF) kernel. densratio() performs following: Decides kernel parameter sigma cross-validation, Optimizes kernel weights theta (words, find optimal coefficients linear model), parameters sigma theta saved densratio object, used compute density ratio call compute_density_ratio().

title: Density Ratio Estimation
text: densratio() outputs result like follows: Kernel type fixed Gaussian. Number kernels number kernels linear model. can change setting kernel_num argument. default, kernel_num = 100. Bandwidth (sigma) Gaussian kernel bandwidth. default, sigma = "auto", algorithm automatically select optimal value cross validation. set sigma number, used. set sigma numeric vector, algorithm select optimal value cross validation. Centers centers Gaussian kernels linear model. selected random data sample x1 underlying numerator distribution p(x). can find whole values result$kernel_info$centers. Kernel Weights theta parameters linear kernel model. can find values result$kernel_weights. Function Estimate Density Ratio named compute_density_ratio().
code: #>  #> Call: #> densratio(x1 = x1, x2 = x2, method = "uLSIF") #>  #> Kernel Information: #>   Kernel type:  Gaussian  #>   Number of kernels:  100  #>   Bandwidth (sigma):  0.1  #>   Centers:  num [1:100, 1] 0.907 1.093 1.18 1.136 1.046 ... #>  #> Kernel Weights: #>   num [1:100] 0.067455 0.040045 0.000459 0.016849 0.067084 ... #>  #> Regularization Parameter (lambda):  1  #>  #> Function to Estimate Density Ratio: #>   compute_density_ratio()

title: Density Ratio Estimation
text: far, input data samples x1 x2 one dimensional. densratio() allows input multidimensional data samples matrix, long dimensions . example, case, well, can compare true density ratio estimated density ratio.
code: library(densratio) library(mvtnorm)  set.seed(3) x1 <- rmvnorm(300, mean = c(1, 1), sigma = diag(1/8, 2)) x2 <- rmvnorm(300, mean = c(1, 1), sigma = diag(1/2, 2))  densratio_obj_2d <- densratio(x1, x2) densratio_obj_2d #>  #> Call: #> densratio(x1 = x1, x2 = x2, method = "uLSIF") #>  #> Kernel Information: #>   Kernel type:  Gaussian  #>   Number of kernels:  100  #>   Bandwidth (sigma):  0.316  #>   Centers:  num [1:100, 1:2] 1.257 0.758 1.122 1.3 1.386 ... #>  #> Kernel Weights: #>   num [1:100] 0.0756 0.0986 0.059 0.0797 0.0421 ... #>  #> Regularization Parameter (lambda):  0.3162278  #>  #> Function to Estimate Density Ratio: #>   compute_density_ratio() true_density_ratio <- function(x) {   dmvnorm(x, mean = c(1, 1), sigma = diag(1/8, 2)) /     dmvnorm(x, mean = c(1, 1), sigma = diag(1/2, 2)) }  N <- 20 range <- seq(0, 2, length.out = N) input <- expand.grid(range, range) w_true <- matrix(true_density_ratio(input), nrow = N) w_hat <- matrix(densratio_obj_2d$compute_density_ratio(input), nrow = N)  par(mfrow = c(1, 2)) contour(range, range, w_true, main = "True Density Ratio") contour(range, range, w_hat, main = "Estimated Density Ratio")

title: Density Ratio Estimation
text: densratio package used several research papers, including: Fricke, K.R., & Herzberg, P.Y. (2019). Know big data: De-biasing subsamples large datasets personality research using importance sampling kNN matching. Chen, M.F., Goel, K., Sohoni, N.S., Poms, F., Fatahalian, K., & R’e, C. (2021). Mandoline: Model Evaluation Distribution Shift. International Conference Machine Learning. Evans, C., & G’Sell, M. (2024). Sequential label shift detection classification data: application dengue fever. PLOS ONE, 19. Chakraborty, ., Huan, X., & Catanach, T.. (2024). Likelihood-Free Approach Goal-Oriented Bayesian Optimal Experimental Design. Awaya, N., Xu, Y., & Ma, L. (2025). Two-sample comparison additive tree models density ratios.

text: x1 numeric vector matrix. Data numerator distribution p(x). x2 numeric vector matrix. Data denominator distribution q(x). method "uLSIF" (default), "RuLSIF", "KLIEP". sigma positive numeric vector. Search range Gaussian kernel bandwidth. lambda positive numeric vector. Search range regularization parameter uLSIF RuLSIF. alpha numeric [0, 1]. Relative parameter RuLSIF. Default 0.1. kernel_num positive integer. Number kernels. fold positive integer. Numer folds cross validation KLIEP. verbose logical (default TRUE).

title: Estimate Density Ratio p(x)/q(x) — densratio
text: densratio object contains function compute estimated density ratio.

title: Estimate Density Ratio p(x)/q(x) — densratio
code: x1 <- rnorm(200, mean = 1, sd = 1/8) x2 <- rnorm(200, mean = 1, sd = 1/2)  densratio_obj <- densratio(x1, x2) #> ################## Start RuLSIF ################## #> Searching optimal sigma and lambda... #>   sigma = 0.001, lambda = 0.001, score = 15.067 #>   sigma = 0.001, lambda = 0.003, score = 0.247 #>   sigma = 0.001, lambda = 0.010, score = -0.550 #>   sigma = 0.003, lambda = 0.010, score = -1.048 #>   sigma = 0.010, lambda = 0.032, score = -1.118 #>   sigma = 0.032, lambda = 0.316, score = -1.138 #>   sigma = 0.100, lambda = 0.032, score = -1.157 #>   sigma = 0.100, lambda = 0.100, score = -1.186 #>   sigma = 0.100, lambda = 0.316, score = -1.207 #>   sigma = 0.100, lambda = 1.000, score = -1.224 #> Found optimal sigma = 0.100, lambda = 1.000. #> Optimizing kernel weights... #> End. #> ################## Finished RuLSIF ###############  new_x <- seq(0, 2, by = 0.05) estimated_density_ratio <- densratio_obj$compute_density_ratio(new_x)  plot(new_x, estimated_density_ratio, pch=19)
